political.s: GM donated $3.2M in 2024 with 77% to Republicans per FEC data, indicating right-leaning donations. The company spent $14.2M on federal lobbying in 2024, primarily on automotive industry issues, supply chain, and emissions standards.

charity.s: GM eliminated its philanthropic foundation in 2017, redirecting $30M annually to corporate giving focused on STEM education and global development. The company operates employee giving programs and donated 59 BrightDrop vans to Red Cross disaster relief efforts.
charity.focus: STEM education, disaster relief, community development

environment.s: In 2024, GM paid $146M in penalties after EPA found 5.9 million vehicles emitted over 10% more CO2 than reported. The company has committed to eliminating tailpipe emissions by 2035 but faces ongoing compliance issues with emissions standards and fuel economy regulations.

labor.s: GM maintains collective bargaining agreements with UAW and has ergonomics programs addressing cumulative trauma disorders since 1990. The company is unionized with generally positive labor relations, though 2018 reports indicated tensions over plant closures.

dei.s: GM quietly removed explicit DEI language from its 2025 10-K filing after mentioning it prominently in 2024, following broader industry trend influenced by Trump administration pressure. The company maintains a dedicated DEI webpage highlighting achievements but shifted language in regulatory filings.

animals.s: Not applicable for automotive manufacturing company.

guns.s: Not applicable for automotive manufacturing company.

privacy.s: In May 2026, GM paid $12.75M in California's largest CCPA penalty for collecting and selling driver location and behavior data through OnStar without proper consent. The company had previously made ~$20M from unlawful data sales between 2020-2024 to insurance companies and data brokers.

execPay.s: CEO Mary Barra earned $29.9M in 2025 (up from $29.5M in 2024), with $21.6M in stock awards. President Mark Reuss earned $19.3M. Executive compensation significantly exceeds worker compensation, indicating substantial pay disparity.

fec.committeeName: GENERAL MOTORS COMPANY POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE (GM PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
